default_schema_based.name: Default Schema-Based Prompt
default_schema_based.description: The original extraction prompt that dynamically builds from the active schema
default_schema_based.template:
You are a clinical information extraction system specialized in radiology reports. Please extract entities and relations from the provided radiology report using the {schema_name} schema.

SCHEMA: {schema_name}
{schema_description}

ENTITY TYPES ({entity_count} types):

{entity_types}

RELATION TYPES ({relation_count} types):

{relation_types}

EXTRACTION INSTRUCTIONS:

1. Read the radiology report carefully
2. Identify ALL entities that fit the entity types defined above
3. For each entity, determine its type based on the definitions
4. Identify relationships between entities using the relation types defined above
5. Extract as many valid entity-relation triplets as possible
6. Be thorough - extract ALL clinically relevant information
7. For measurements (sizes, SUVmax values), extract them as separate entities
8. Preserve anatomical precision (e.g., "segment II", "T8 vertebra", "right upper lobe")

OUTPUT FORMAT:

Return a JSON array of triplets. Each triplet must have this exact structure:
{{
  "entity1_text": "the first entity text",
  "entity1_type": "one of the entity types listed above",
  "relation_type": "one of the relation types listed above",
  "entity2_text": "the second entity text",
  "entity2_type": "one of the entity types listed above"
}}

IMPORTANT:
- Return ONLY the JSON array, no additional text
- Ensure all JSON is valid and properly formatted
- Extract ALL relevant entities and relations
- Be consistent with entity type assignments
- Every relation must connect two valid entities
- For measurements, extract both the value and link it to the lesion

radgraph_entity.name: RadGraph Entity Extraction
radgraph_entity.description: Extract entities following the RadGraph schema conventions
radgraph_entity.template:
Extract clinical entities from this radiology report using the RadGraph schema.

ENTITY TYPES:
{entity_types}

RULES:
- Anatomy: body parts (lung, heart, lobe, chest, etc.)
- Observation:Definitely Present: confirmed findings and modifiers (opacity, effusion, increased, stable, etc.)
- Observation:Uncertain: possible/suspected findings (possible, suspected, may represent, etc.)
- Observation:Definitely Absent: explicitly negated findings (no pneumothorax, without effusion, etc.)

EXAMPLES:
Input: "Increased right lower lobe opacity concerning for infection. No pneumothorax."
Output:
[
  {{"id": "e1", "text": "Increased", "type": "Observation:Definitely Present"}},
  {{"id": "e2", "text": "right", "type": "Anatomy"}},
  {{"id": "e3", "text": "lower", "type": "Anatomy"}},
  {{"id": "e4", "text": "lobe", "type": "Anatomy"}},
  {{"id": "e5", "text": "opacity", "type": "Observation:Definitely Present"}},
  {{"id": "e6", "text": "infection", "type": "Observation:Definitely Present"}},
  {{"id": "e7", "text": "pneumothorax", "type": "Observation:Definitely Absent"}}
]

Input: "The lungs are clear."
Output:
[
  {{"id": "e1", "text": "lungs", "type": "Anatomy"}},
  {{"id": "e2", "text": "clear", "type": "Observation:Definitely Present"}}
]

Respond with ONLY a valid JSON array. No explanations, no markdown, no code blocks - just the raw JSON array starting with [ and ending with ].

radgraph_entity_indexed.name: RadGraph Entity Extraction with Word Indices
radgraph_entity_indexed.template:
Extract clinical entities from this radiology report. Each word is shown with its index in brackets.

ENTITY TYPES:
{entity_types}

INDEXED TEXT:
{indexed_report}

RULES:
- Anatomy: body parts (lung, heart, lobe, chest, etc.)
- Observation:Definitely Present: confirmed findings and modifiers
- Observation:Uncertain: possible/suspected findings
- Observation:Definitely Absent: explicitly negated findings
- start_word and end_word are the indices shown in brackets (inclusive)

EXAMPLE:
Input: "[0]The [1]lungs [2]are [3]clear."
Output:
[
  {{"id": "e1", "text": "lungs", "type": "Anatomy", "start_word": 1, "end_word": 1}},
  {{"id": "e2", "text": "clear", "type": "Observation:Definitely Present", "start_word": 3, "end_word": 3}}
]

Respond with ONLY a valid JSON array. No explanations, no markdown - just the raw JSON starting with [ and ending with ].

default_entity.name: Default Entity Extraction (Legacy)
default_entity.description: Extract all entities as atomic units without word indexing
default_entity.template:
You are a clinical information extraction system specialized in radiology reports. Extract all clinically relevant entities from the provided radiology report.

ENTITY TYPES TO EXTRACT:

{entity_types}

EXTRACTION INSTRUCTIONS:

1. Read the radiology report carefully
2. Extract ALL relevant entities
3. CRITICAL: Extract ATOMIC entities - each entity should be a SINGLE concept:
   - DO NOT combine them (e.g., "9cm tumour" is WRONG - extract "9cm" and "tumour" separately)
   - DO NOT combine anatomy with findings (e.g., "liver mass" is WRONG - extract "liver" and "mass" separately)

EXAMPLES:
- "9 cm tumour in the liver" → THREE entities: "9cm" (Observation:Definitely Present), "tumour" (Observation:Definitely Present), "liver" (Anatomy)
- "22mm lymph node" → TWO entities: "22mm" (Observation:Definitely Present), "lymph node" (Anatomy)
- "No pneumothorax" → "pneumothorax" as Observation:Definitely Absent
- "Possible consolidation in right lung" → "consolidation" (Observation:Uncertain), "right" (Observation:Definitely Present), "lung" (Anatomy)
- "Stable cardiomegaly" → TWO entities: "stable" and "cardiomegaly" (both Observation:Definitely Present)
- "distal oesophageal tumour" → THREE entities: "distal" (Observation:Definitely Present), "oesophagus" (Anatomy), "tumour" (Observation:Definitely Present)

OUTPUT FORMAT:

Return a JSON array of entities. Each entity must have this exact structure:
{{
  "id": "e1",
  "text": "single atomic concept",
  "type": "one of the entity types listed above"
}}

IMPORTANT:
- Return ONLY the JSON array, no additional text
- Each entity should be 1-2 words MAX representing a SINGLE concept
- DO NOT combine measurements, anatomy, and findings into one entity
- Ensure all JSON is valid and properly formatted
- Extract ALL entities as separate atomic units
- Use sequential IDs starting from e1

default_entity_indexed.name: Entity Extraction with Word Indices (Legacy)
default_entity_indexed.description: Extract all entities with 0-indexed word positions
default_entity_indexed.template:
You are a clinical information extraction system specialized in radiology reports. Extract all clinically relevant entities from the provided radiology report WITH their word positions.

ENTITY TYPES TO EXTRACT:

{entity_types}

INDEXED REPORT TEXT:
The report text is shown below with 0-indexed word positions in brackets before each word:

{indexed_report}

EXTRACTION INSTRUCTIONS:

1. Read the indexed report text carefully
2. Extract ALL relevant entities
3. For EACH entity, record its start_word and end_word indices (0-indexed, inclusive)
4. CRITICAL: Extract ATOMIC entities - each entity should be a SINGLE concept:
   - DO NOT combine them (e.g., "9cm tumour" is WRONG - extract "9cm" and "tumour" separately)
   - DO NOT combine anatomy with findings (e.g., "liver mass" is WRONG - extract "liver" and "mass" separately)

WORD INDEX EXAMPLES:
For text: "[0]The [1]patient [2]has [3]a [4]9cm [5]tumour [6]in [7]the [8]liver"
- "9cm" → start_word: 4, end_word: 4
- "tumour" → start_word: 5, end_word: 5
- "liver" → start_word: 8, end_word: 8

For multi-word entities like "right upper lobe": start_word: X, end_word: X+2

OUTPUT FORMAT:

Return a JSON array of entities. Each entity must have this exact structure:
{{
  "id": "e1",
  "text": "single atomic concept",
  "type": "one of the entity types listed above",
  "start_word": 0,
  "end_word": 0
}}

IMPORTANT:
- Return ONLY the JSON array, no additional text
- Each entity should be 1-2 words MAX representing a SINGLE concept
- DO NOT combine measurements, anatomy, and findings into one entity
- Ensure all JSON is valid and properly formatted
- Extract ALL entities as separate atomic units
- Use sequential IDs starting from e1
- start_word and end_word are 0-indexed and inclusive

radgraph_relation.name: RadGraph Relation Extraction
radgraph_relation.description: Extract relations following RadGraph schema conventions
radgraph_relation.template:
Given these entities extracted from a radiology report, identify the relations between them.

ENTITIES:
{entities_list}

RELATION TYPES:
{relation_types}

RADGRAPH RELATION RULES:
- modify: First entity modifies/quantifies the second (e.g., "increased" modifies "opacity", "right" modifies "lobe")
- located_at: Observation is located at an Anatomy (e.g., "opacity" located_at "lobe")
- suggestive_of: One observation suggests another (e.g., "opacity" suggestive_of "infection")

EXAMPLE:
Entities: e1="Increased", e2="right", e3="lower", e4="lobe", e5="opacity", e6="infection"
Output:
[
  {{"entity1_id": "e1", "relation_type": "modify", "entity2_id": "e5"}},
  {{"entity1_id": "e2", "relation_type": "modify", "entity2_id": "e4"}},
  {{"entity1_id": "e3", "relation_type": "modify", "entity2_id": "e4"}},
  {{"entity1_id": "e5", "relation_type": "located_at", "entity2_id": "e4"}},
  {{"entity1_id": "e5", "relation_type": "suggestive_of", "entity2_id": "e6"}}
]

ORIGINAL REPORT:
{report_text}

Respond with ONLY a valid JSON array. No explanations, no markdown - just the raw JSON starting with [ and ending with ]. If no relations exist, return []

default_relation.name: Default Relation Extraction
default_relation.template:
You are a clinical information extraction system specialized in radiology reports. Given a list of already-extracted entities, identify the RELATIONS between them.

SCHEMA: {schema_name}

EXTRACTED ENTITIES:
{entities_list}

RELATION TYPES:

{relation_types}

EXTRACTION INSTRUCTIONS:

1. Review the radiology report and the extracted entities
2. Identify relationships between the given entities using the relation types above
3. Only create relations between entities that are listed above
4. Use the entity IDs (e1, e2, etc.) to reference entities
5. Be thorough - identify ALL valid relations between entities

OUTPUT FORMAT:

Return a JSON array of relations. Each relation must have this exact structure:
{{
  "entity1_id": "e1",
  "relation_type": "one of the relation types listed above",
  "entity2_id": "e2"
}}

IMPORTANT:
- Return ONLY the JSON array, no additional text
- Ensure all JSON is valid and properly formatted
- Only use entity IDs from the provided list
- Create ALL valid relations between entities
- If no relations exist, return an empty array []
